ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for papaya crispy pork belly salad specifically. FOODON_00002809 (pork dish) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Pork meat products) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for this composite salad dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for this specific composite salad dish. SNOMED CT does not have a suitable food concept at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of approximately 350 g, comprising a bowl of fresh papaya, mixed greens, and sliced crispy pork belly with dressing.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: USDA FoodData Central — Pork belly (raw, cooked), Papaya (raw), Mixed salad greens; cross-referenced with HPB Singapore nutrient data for analogous pork and fruit salad dishes
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: approximately 40% crispy pork belly (cooked, USDA FDC #168289 pork belly cooked), 35% fresh papaya (USDA FDC #169926), 20% mixed salad greens (USDA FDC #169248), and 5% dressing/seasoning. Values represent a plausible per-100g composite. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of papaya and mixed salad with meat — analogous estimate based on: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for papaya (GI ~58) and pork (GI ~0, protein-fat dominant).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on weighted contribution of components: papaya has a GI of approximately 58 (University of Sydney GI Database), but its contribution is moderated by the high fat and protein content of crispy pork belly (GI effectively 0 for protein/fat-dominant foods) and the fibre from mixed greens. The composite dish GI is estimated at 45 (low category, ≤55). Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 27.3) / 100 ≈ 12.3. Serving carbohydrate = 7.8 g/100g × 350 g serving = 27.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 320 mg per 100 g; a 350 g serving delivers approximately 1120 mg sodium, representing nearly half the recommended daily limit of 2300 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this dish in moderation and request reduced-sodium dressing, as the crispy pork belly and seasoning contribute significant sodium that may elevate blood pressure with regular consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Estimated glycemic index of 45 (low) and glycemic load of approximately 12.3 per serving; however, the moderate carbohydrate content from papaya and the high fat content from pork belly require portion awareness.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can include this salad in a balanced meal plan given its low estimated GI, but should be mindful of the high saturated fat from crispy pork belly and the natural sugars from papaya, and limit portion size to one standard serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (13.5 g/100g) and saturated fat (4.8 g/100g) from crispy pork belly, combined with cholesterol (42 mg/100g) and elevated sodium, present multiple cardiovascular risk factors.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dish due to its high saturated fat and sodium content from the crispy pork belly, and consider requesting a smaller portion of pork with extra greens and papaya to improve the nutritional profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Papaya Crispy Pork Belly Salad (木瓜脆皮三层肉沙拉) is a contemporary Singapore restaurant salad dish that combines fresh ripe or semi-ripe papaya with crispy roasted pork belly (siu yuk-style three-layer pork) and mixed fresh greens, typically dressed with a light vinaigrette or Asian-style dressing. The dish is served at Viet'Spice restaurant on Circular Road, Singapore, and reflects a fusion of Southeast Asian and Vietnamese culinary influences within the Singapore dining scene. The pork belly is roasted until the skin achieves a crackling, crispy texture while the meat layers remain tender and juicy, providing a satisfying contrast of textures. The papaya contributes natural sweetness, a soft yielding texture, and a bright colour, while the fresh greens add crunch and freshness. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense due to the fat content of pork belly, providing approximately 185 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein (9.2 g/100g) and moderate carbohydrates (7.8 g/100g) primarily from the papaya. The dish is a good source of vitamin C from papaya (approximately 28 mg/100g) and provides B vitamins from the pork. The estimated glycemic index is 45 (low category), making it relatively suitable for blood glucose management when consumed in appropriate portions. Clinically, the primary concerns are the elevated sodium from seasoning and pork preparation, and the saturated fat from the pork belly, which are relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is not suitable for halal or vegetarian diets. It is best consumed as a main course salad and is priced at SGD 18.90 per serving.

confidence_reason: No exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary approved source for this specific composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central data for individual ingredients (pork belly cooked, papaya raw, mixed greens). GI is estimated by analogy from University of Sydney GI Database values for papaya and protein/fat-dominant foods. Confidence is medium due to the estimation methodology and the absence of a direct laboratory analysis or primary database entry for this dish.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the approved source database (USDA FoodData Central) for the components of this dish, preventing reliable aggregation. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B vitamins, folate, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water are estimated from component USDA FDC data and should be treated as approximate. The food_group has been corrected from 'Vegetables' (input) to 'Mixed' as this dish contains substantial meat (pork belly) and cannot be classified solely as a vegetable dish. The dish is served at Viet'Spice - Hương vị Việt - Circular Road, Singapore, at SGD 18.90. The menu item code SL2 is preserved in aliases. glycemic_index is estimated at 45 (low, ≤55) based on analogous component GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). No zero-value core macros are present in this record.
